panels.title: Head Slot
panels.description: Latest slot of the beacon chain head. Should advance by 1 every ~12 seconds (one slot). If it lags the wall-clock slot, this node is falling behind and cannot attest or propose.

panels.title: Finalized Epoch
panels.description: Most recent epoch that has been economically finalized by a 2/3+ validator supermajority. Under normal conditions this sits 2 epochs behind the current epoch. A widening gap (> 4 epochs) signals finality is delayed — typically caused by participation issues or a minority client bug.

panels.title: Current Justified Epoch
panels.description: The most recent epoch that has received 2/3+ attesting support. Justification is the step before finalization; this should normally equal `current_epoch - 1`.

panels.title: Previous Justified Epoch
panels.description: The second-most-recent justified epoch. Shown alongside current justified so you can see the justification cursor advance epoch-by-epoch.

panels.title: Head Slot over time
panels.description: Latest slot of the beacon chain head. Should advance by 1 every ~12 seconds (one slot). If it lags the wall-clock slot, this node is falling behind and cannot attest or propose.

panels.title: Finalized Epoch over time
panels.description: Most recent epoch that has been economically finalized by a 2/3+ validator supermajority. Under normal conditions this sits 2 epochs behind the current epoch. A widening gap (> 4 epochs) signals finality is delayed — typically caused by participation issues or a minority client bug.

panels.title: Current Justified Epoch over time
panels.description: The most recent epoch that has received 2/3+ attesting support. Justification is the step before finalization; this should normally equal `current_epoch - 1`.

panels.title: Previous Justified Epoch over time
panels.description: The second-most-recent justified epoch. Shown alongside current justified so you can see the justification cursor advance epoch-by-epoch.

panels.title: Active Validators
panels.description: Current total active validators in the active set this epoch — i.e. validators eligible to attest and propose. Universal across all clients.

panels.title: Total Validators
panels.description:
Count of all validators in status `pending | active | exited | withdrawable` in the current epoch — i.e. the total validator registry size.

**Coverage gap**: Prysm does not expose a total-validator count. Lighthouse, Teku, Nimbus and Grandine approximate with related metrics — values may differ slightly from the strict spec semantics.

panels.title: Active Validators over time
panels.description: Current total active validators in the active set this epoch — i.e. validators eligible to attest and propose. Universal across all clients.

panels.title: Total Validators over time

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  
  max by (instance) (
    beacon_current_validators
  or on() last_over_time(beacon_head_state_total_validators_total[5m])
  or on() last_over_time(beacon_current_active_validators[5m])
  ),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description:
Count of all validators in status `pending | active | exited | withdrawable` in the current epoch — i.e. the total validator registry size.

**Coverage gap**: Prysm does not expose a total-validator count. Lighthouse, Teku, Nimbus and Grandine approximate with related metrics — values may differ slightly from the strict spec semantics.

panels.title: libp2p Peers
panels.description: Current count of libp2p peers connected to this beacon node. A healthy node typically maintains 30–100 peers. Sustained drops usually mean networking issues, a misconfigured port, or peer-scoring problems.

panels.title: Gossipsub Mesh Peers

targets.expr:
sum(gossipsub_mesh_peer_counts)
  or on() (sum(last_over_time(p2p_topic_peer_count[5m])))
  or on() (sum(last_over_time(network_subnet_peer_count[5m])))
  or on() (sum(last_over_time(libp2p_gossipsub_peers_per_topic_mesh[5m])))
  or on() (sum(last_over_time(gossipsub_mesh_peer_count[5m])))
  or on() (sum(last_over_time(gossipsub_mesh_peer_inclusion_events_subscribed_total[5m])))

panels.description:
Number of partial-message-capable peers in the gossipsub mesh, summed across all topics. Mesh quality determines how fast this node sees new blocks and attestations — low counts lead to block-delay spikes.

**Caveat**: Teku does not expose a direct gossipsub mesh peer gauge. On Teku this panel falls back to `network_subnet_peer_count`, which counts subnet peers rather than per-topic mesh peers — similar magnitude but different semantics.

panels.title: libp2p Peers over time

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  sum by (instance) (libp2p_peers)
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(connected_libp2p_peers[5m])),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description: Current count of libp2p peers connected to this beacon node. A healthy node typically maintains 30–100 peers. Sustained drops usually mean networking issues, a misconfigured port, or peer-scoring problems.

panels.title: Gossipsub Mesh Peers over time

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  sum by (instance) (gossipsub_mesh_peer_counts)
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(p2p_topic_peer_count[5m]))
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(network_subnet_peer_count[5m]))
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(libp2p_gossipsub_peers_per_topic_mesh[5m]))
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(gossipsub_mesh_peer_count[5m]))
    or on(instance) sum by (instance) (last_over_time(gossipsub_mesh_peer_inclusion_events_subscribed_total[5m])),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description: Number of partial-message-capable peers in the gossipsub mesh, summed across all topics. Mesh quality determines how fast this node sees new blocks and attestations — low counts lead to block-delay spikes.

panels.title: Reorgs Total (cumulative)

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  
  max by (instance) (
    beacon_reorgs_total
  or on() last_over_time(beacon_fork_choice_changed_head_total[5m])
  ),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description: Cumulative count of chain reorganizations observed by this node (fork-choice head changes). Small numbers (< 5 per day) are normal; sudden spikes indicate network-wide consensus instability or this node being on a minority fork.

panels.title: Deposits Total (cumulative)

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  
  max by (instance) (
    beacon_processed_deposits_total
  or on() last_over_time(beacon_eth1_deposit_total[5m])
  ),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description:
Number of validator deposits processed from the deposit contract on the execution layer.

**Note**: This is a **since-start** counter — it reflects deposits observed by the running beacon-node process, not a genesis-cumulative total. Restarting the BN resets the value to 0 until new deposits occur. Verified on Lighthouse (`beacon_processed_deposits_total`) and Teku (`beacon_eth1_deposit_total`) — both have this semantic.

panels.title: Block Delay (p90)

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(block_arrival_latency_milliseconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance)) / 1000
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(beacon_block_delay_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance))
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(lodestar_gossip_block_elapsed_time_till_received_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance))
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(beacon_block_gossip_slot_start_delay_time_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance)),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description:
90th-percentile block arrival delay relative to the expected slot start. Values above 4 s mean blocks are consistently late to this node — could be a sync issue, poor peer quality, or slow propagation.

**Coverage gap**: Lighthouse and Teku do not expose a histogram-based block-delay metric. The panel will be empty when either is running. Prysm reports delay in milliseconds (normalised to seconds by the panel). Nimbus, Lodestar and Grandine expose native second-based histograms.

panels.title: State Data Cache Misses (rate)

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  
  sum by (instance) (
    rate(beacon_state_data_cache_misses_total[5m])
  or on() rate(store_beacon_historic_state_cache_miss_total[5m])
  or on() rate(hot_state_cache_miss[5m])
  or on() rate(storage_block_cache_data_miss[5m])
  or on() rate(beacon_state_data_cache_misses[5m])
  ),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description:
Rate of state-data cache misses per second. High rates indicate cache pressure — the beacon node is falling back to disk for state reads, which slows down attestation processing and head-block updates.

**Coverage gap**: Grandine does not expose a state-cache miss metric; panel will be empty on Grandine.

**Caveat**: Lodestar's fallback (`lodestar_state_cache_size`) is cache *size*, not miss *count* — its numeric meaning differs from the other clients.

panels.title: Engine getBlobs Request Dur (p90)

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(beacon_engine_getBlobsV3_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance))
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(beacon_engine_getBlobsV2_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance))
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(execution_layer_request_times_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance))
  or on() histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(engine_api_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, instance)),
  "cl", "$2", "instance", "(cl-)?([a-z]+):[0-9]+"
)

panels.description:
90th-percentile latency of `engine_getBlobsV3` requests to the execution layer. Elevated latency can indicate slow EL processing, network congestion, or EL saturation.

**Coverage gap**: Prysm does not expose this metric; panel will be empty on Prysm.

**Caveat**: Lighthouse and Nimbus fall back to generic engine-API latency metrics rather than blob-specific ones, so their values include non-blob engine calls too.

panels.title: Peers by Client Type
panels.description:
Current count of connected libp2p peers grouped by the remote peer's client-type.

**Caveat**: Lighthouse, Prysm and Lodestar expose per-peer-type metrics. Teku, Nimbus and Grandine do not — panel will be empty on those clients.

panels.title: Peers by Client Type over time
panels.description:
History of connected libp2p peers grouped by the remote peer's client-type. Useful for tracking whether one client type is disappearing from your peer list over time.

**Caveat**: Lighthouse, Prysm and Lodestar expose per-peer-type metrics. Teku, Nimbus and Grandine do not — panel will be empty on those clients.
Status returned from the `/readyz` health status endpoint. Unhealthy reasons include beacon node problems, charon p2p problems, or validator client problems.
panels.title: Health Status - Peer

panels.description: Successful cluster duty percentage by type over the whole time range
panels.title: Cluster Duty Success(%)

panels.description:
Information about the charon cluster.

- **Network**: The blockchain network 
- **Operators/Nodes**: The number of operators or nodes or peers in the charon DV cluster.
- **Threshold**: The minimum number of participating nodes (also quorum).
- **Validators**: The number of validators added to the cluster, each validator is staking 32 ETH.

targets.expr:
( 1 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="mainnet"} )
or 
( 2 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="goerli"} )
or
( 3 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="sepolia"} )
or
( 4 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="gnosis"} )

panels.title: Cluster Info

panels.description: Health checks that have failed at least once in the selected time window, based on the app_health_checks_failed_total counter.
panels.title: Health Checks History

panels.description: Status returned from the `/readyz` health status endpoint. Unhealthy reasons include beacon node problems, charon p2p problems, or validator client problems.
panels.title: Historical Health Status

panels.description: Completed duties by type over time.
panels.title: ✅ Completed duties by type

panels.description:
Failed duties by type over time. Use data link to show logs.
Proposer duties are non-failing after being successfully included on-chain.
Other broadcasted to the beacon node duties (i.e.: attester), are non-failing after being successfully broadcasted.
Any other duties not broadcasted to the beacon node (i.e.: randao), are non-failing after consensus is reached.
panels.title: ❌ Failed duties by type (per $interval)

panels.description: Failed duties percentage by type over time
panels.title: ❌ Failed duty percentage by type (per $interval)

panels.description: Number of failed duties by reason per time window. Note this is only available from v0.16.
panels.title: 🚨 Duty failure reason: $duty (per $interval)

panels.description: Duties that was successfully broadcast to the Beacon Node, but that was never included on-chain. This could be due to broadcasting too late (too slow), but it could also just be to aspects of consensus layer out-of-our-control. See https://eth2book.info/bellatrix/part2/incentives/rewards/#remarks.
panels.title: 💥 Broadcasted duties never included on-chain (per $interval)

panels.description: Number of consensus timeouts per time window.
panels.title: 🚨 Consensus Timeouts (per $interval)

panels.description:
Type of connections to each peer in the cluster during the time window:
 - 🟢 Green: Direct connection (this is the preferred)
 - 🟡 Yellow: Indirect relay connection (this is ok if only temporary)
 - 🔴 Red: No connection to peer (this is a problem)

See https://docs.obol.tech/docs/charon/networking#external-p2p-network
panels.title: Peer Connections (🟢=direct, 🟡=relay, 🔴=not connected)
